mnemonics.title: Critical Section Requirements
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER THE THREE REQUIREMENTS AS 'MPB': MUTUAL EXCLUSION, PROGRESS, BOUNDED WAITING. THINK: 'MY PROCESSES MUST BE FAIR' (M-Mutual, P-Progress, B-Bound).

mnemonics.title: Peterson's Solution Variables
mnemonics.content: TURN (WHO'S TURN?) + FLAG (WHO WANTS?) = PETERSON'S SOLUTION. THINK: 'TWO FLAGS AND A TURN.'

mnemonics.title: Process Sections Order
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER ORDER: ENTRY → CRITICAL → EXIT → REMAINDER. ACRONYM: E-C-E-R (EVERY CRITICAL EXIT REQUIRES ENTRY).

acronyms.expansion: Critical Section - the portion of code accessing shared resources
acronyms.usage: Multiple processes compete to enter their CS to access shared variables safely

acronyms.expansion: Mutual Exclusion, Progress, Bounded Waiting - the three requirements
acronyms.usage: Any valid solution to the critical section problem must satisfy all three MPB requirements

acronyms.expansion: Race Condition - outcome depends on timing
acronyms.usage: RC occurs when multiple processes access shared data concurrently without proper synchronization
TS instruction is used to implement atomic locks for mutual exclusion

visualTips.visualization: VISUALIZE TWO PEOPLE TRYING TO USE ONE DOOR: FIRST THEY BOTH SAY 'I WANT TO GO' (SET FLAG), THEN THEY DECIDE WHO GOES BASED ON WHOSE TURN IT IS. PICTURE A DOOR WITH A SIGN THAT SHOWS WHOSE TURN IT IS AND TWO HANDS RAISED SHOWING INTENT.

visualTips.visualize: THINK OF A SINGLE TOILET IN A RESTROOM: ONLY ONE PERSON CAN USE IT AT A TIME. THE LOCK ON THE DOOR REPRESENTS THE MUTUAL EXCLUSION MECHANISM.
PICTURE A SECURITY GUARD WHO ATOMICALLY (INSTANTLY) CHECKS IF THE DOOR IS LOCKED AND SETS IT TO LOCKED IN ONE MOTION, RETURNING WHETHER IT WAS PREVIOUSLY UNLOCKED.
THINK OF A DELI COUNTER WITH A NUMBERING SYSTEM. AFTER TAKING A NUMBER, YOU KNOW AT MOST HOW MANY PEOPLE WILL BE SERVED BEFORE YOU (BOUND). THIS PREVENTS STARVATION.

analogies.analogy: THINK OF A BANK ACCOUNT. MULTIPLE ATMs TRYING TO WITHDRAW FROM THE SAME ACCOUNT SIMULTANEOUSLY. THE WITHDRAWAL CODE IS THE CRITICAL SECTION - ONLY ONE WITHDRAWAL SHOULD HAPPEN AT A TIME TO PREVENT INCORRECT BALANCES.
IMAGINE TWO PEOPLE TRYING TO UPDATE A SHARED DOCUMENT ON GOOGLE DOCS AT THE SAME TIME. WHOSE CHANGES GET SAVED DEPENDS ON TIMING - THIS IS A RACE CONDITION.
THINK OF A QUEUE AT A BUS STOP. BOUNDED WAITING IS LIKE A RULE SAYING 'IF YOU WAIT FOR MORE THAN 5 BUSES TO PASS, YOU GET PRIORITY.' THIS PREVENTS SOMEONE FROM WAITING FOREVER.

analogies.analize: IMAGINE A MEETING WHERE IF NO ONE IS SPEAKING (CRITICAL SECTION EMPTY) AND SOMEONE WANTS TO SPEAK, THEY SHOULD BE ABLE TO START WITHOUT UNNECESSARY DELAY. PEOPLE ALREADY FINISHED TALKING (NOT IN REMAINDER) GET TO DECIDE.
